name: behave-the-biology-of-humans-at-our-best-and-worst
description: Robert Sapolsky's 'Behave: The Biology of Humans at Our Best and Worst' — a monumental exploration of why humans do what they do. Organized by time scale: from one second before (brain) to millennia before (evolution). Covers aggression, morality, empathy, hierarchy, criminal justice, free will, and war and peace.
